samples.text: A train can be moving while a passenger inside is sitting still relative to the train. Is this logically consistent?
A train moves while a passenger inside stays still relative to the carriage. Does this make logical sense?
A locomotive can move while a rider inside is stationary relative to the car. Is this logically consistent?
Motion is relative. The passenger is still relative to the train but moving relative to the ground; no contradiction. Is the described situation logically consistent?
A train can be moving while a passenger inside is sitting still relative to the train. The meeting started at noon. Is this logically consistent?
